sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Thompson, Gap Inc chief people officer, sells $628k in stock
feed.summary: Amanda J. Thompson, Chief People Officer of Gap Inc. (NYSE:GAP), sold 25,000 shares of company stock for approximately $628,385 on April 6, 2026. Following the sale, Thompson directly owns 86,228 shares. The stock is currently trading below its InvestingPro Fair Value, and the company has a strong financial health score and a history of dividend payments.

feed.title: FitLife Brands posts Q4 revenue surge to $25.9M after Irwin acquisition; Q4 adjusted EBITDA $3.5M
feed.summary: FitLife Brands reported a significant increase in its fourth-quarter revenue, reaching $25.9 million, primarily due to the acquisition of Irwin. The company also posted a Q4 adjusted EBITDA of $3.5 million, marking a 14% year-over-year increase. While legacy FitLife organic revenue grew for the year, Q4 legacy revenue saw a decline.

feed.title: Raymond James reiterates Market Perform on fuboTV stock after guidance cut
feed.summary: Raymond James reiterated its Market Perform rating on fuboTV (NYSE:FUBO) after the streaming service lowered its fiscal 2026 adjusted EBITDA guidance to $80 million to $100 million, down from previous estimates, and reduced its fiscal 2028 adjusted EBITDA target to at least $300 million. This revised outlook, which implies an 83% EBITDA compound annual growth rate from 2026 to 2028, reflects investor concerns about the company's path to profitability, with the stock declining 62% over the past year. Other analyst adjustments include Citizens raising their price target to $15 and Needham adjusting theirs to $15 following a reverse stock split.

feed.title: Wynn Resorts (WYNN) CFO receives new restricted stock and PSU grants
feed.summary: Wynn Resorts CFO Craig Jeffrey Fullalove has been granted 1,857 performance share units (PSUs) and additional restricted common stock awards. These equity compensation awards, issued under the company's 2014 Omnibus Incentive Plan, vest over several years based on continued service and the achievement of pre-established financial performance goals, as well as total shareholder return. Following these grants, Fullalove holds 25,146 shares of common stock directly and 1,857 PSUs.

feed.title: Tax-driven GoDaddy (NYSE: GDDY) stock sale by strategy and legal chief
feed.summary: GoDaddy's Chief Strategy & Legal Officer, Jared F. Sine, sold 7,793 shares of Class A common stock at $80.71 per share on April 2, 2026. This transaction was to cover tax withholding obligations from vested Restricted Stock Units, as per company policy, and was not a discretionary sale. After the sale, Sine directly holds 77,816 shares of GoDaddy Class A common stock.

feed.title: Nick Khan (NYSE: TKO) sells 9,518 shares under Rule 10b5-1 plan
feed.summary: TKO Group Holdings director Nick Khan sold 9,518 shares of Class A Common Stock on April 6, 2026, through open-market transactions, according to a pre-arranged Rule 10b5-1 trading plan adopted on March 7, 2025. The shares were sold at weighted average prices ranging from $197.12 to $203.84, with Khan retaining approximately 100,618 shares after the sales. This transaction is considered a routine pre-planned sale, and Khan maintains a substantial equity stake in the company.

feed.title: Wynn Resorts (WYNN) CFO discloses 20,835-share equity stake
feed.summary: Wynn Resorts CFO Craig Jeffrey Fullalove has disclosed an initial equity stake of 20,835 shares of common stock. This holding includes 18,662 time-based restricted shares that vest with continued service and 1,475 performance-based restricted shares tied to financial goals for 2026-2028. The disclosure was made via a Form 3 filing, indicating new beneficial ownership rather than recent transactions.

feed.title: Will EOG be Able to Bank on the Ongoing Strength in Crude Prices?
feed.summary: EOG Resources, an upstream oil and gas company, benefits from high crude oil prices due to its portfolio of low production cost assets, primarily in the United States. The favorable market conditions allow EOG to generate enhanced cash flow for exploration projects and strengthen its balance sheet. Other major players like Chevron and Exxon Mobil are also well-positioned to benefit from rising crude prices.

feed.title: Enovix (ENVX) Chief Legal Officer receives 159,362 RSUs, total holdings 611,007
feed.summary: Enovix (ENVX) Chief Legal Officer, Arthi Chakravarthy, was granted 159,362 Restricted Stock Units (RSUs) as an equity award, increasing her total holdings to 611,007 shares. These RSUs, granted at no cash cost, will vest in 12 equal quarterly installments starting April 1, 2026, contingent on her continued service. The total holdings include both RSUs and Performance Restricted Stock Units (PRSUs) with various release schedules up to April 2028.

feed.title: MGM Resorts enters voting agreement with major shareholder, guarantees board seats
feed.summary: MGM Resorts International has reached a voting agreement with its largest shareholder, Barry Diller and IAC Inc., which guarantees IAC two board seats in perpetuity. While IAC can freely vote on the first 25.73% of its holdings, any shares beyond that threshold must be voted proportionally with other shareholders, limiting concentrated voting power. This agreement formalizes a balance between a strategic investor's representation and the company's governance stability, especially significant given MGM's ongoing major projects like the MGM Osaka integrated resort.

feed.title: Southwest adds a free checked wine case as Sonoma flights begin
feed.summary: Southwest Airlines (NYSE: LUV) has launched new nonstop service to Santa Rosa (Charles M. Schulz Sonoma County Airport) and will introduce a "Sip and Ship" program allowing customers to check one case of wine free of charge from select West Coast locations starting April 24, 2026. This expansion adds Santa Rosa as Southwest's 14th intra-California destination and aims to enhance customer convenience for wine country visitors. The airline also reported dividend declarations and Starlink WiFi upgrades, maintaining a focus on operational enhancements and customer-focused perks.

feed.title: Moody's cuts outlook on Blue Owl fund to negative over surge in redemption requests
feed.summary: Moody's Ratings has cut its outlook on Blue Owl's $36 billion Credit Income Corp (OCIC) fund to negative from stable due to "significantly higher" redemption requests in the first quarter compared to its peers. The ratings agency highlighted that a majority of these redemption requests came from a limited number of investors, indicating concentration in the equity holder base. While Blue Owl plans to fulfill only 5% of the 21.9% of shares requested for withdrawal, Moody's expects elevated redemptions to continue and inflows to slow, impacting the fund's capital and liquidity.

feed.title: Trending Industry Today: Norwegian Cruise Leads Gains Post-Market In Cruise Lines Stocks
feed.summary: This article highlights Norwegian Cruise Line as the top gainer in the cruise line industry during post-market trading. The headline suggests a positive trend for the company and potentially the sector.

feed.title: Applied Materials looks to ramp up investment in India’s deep-tech startups
feed.summary: Applied Materials is intensifying its focus on India's deep-tech startup ecosystem through its venture arm, Applied Ventures, to capitalize on the country's maturing semiconductor and deep-tech landscape. The company's CTO, Om Nalamasu, highlighted that increased policy support and early-stage investments are creating a stronger pipeline of startups in India. Applied Ventures has joined the India Deep Tech Alliance (IDTA) as a strategic partner, signaling deeper engagement and a shift towards product development and R&D in India, beyond its historical engineering and design focus.

feed.title: BofA Banker Bettamio to Be CEO Candidate for Brazil’s B3
feed.summary: Alexandre Bettamio of Bank of America Corp. is reportedly being considered as a candidate for the chief executive officer position at Brazil's B3 stock exchange. Internal candidates, such as Luiz Masagao, vice president of products and clients, are also under consideration for the role.

feed.title: News Corp Details Progress of US$1 Billion U.S. Share Repurchase Program
feed.summary: News Corp has provided an update on its US$1 billion share repurchase program, which targets Nasdaq-listed Class A and Class B common stock. The company intends to execute these repurchases from time to time in the open market, excluding ASX-listed CDIs. This move aims to potentially reduce the share count, enhance value for U.S.-listed equity holders, and demonstrates capital management discipline focused on optimizing its U.S. capital structure.

feed.title: Grandview woman asking for help after Evergy power outage
feed.summary: A Grandview woman, Patrice Harris, is seeking help after an Evergy power outage last August reportedly damaged her appliances. Despite an email from Evergy acknowledging equipment failure as the cause of the outage, the company has denied liability for her damaged microwave, dishwasher, refrigerator, and stovetop, citing tariffs that exempt them from damages due to equipment failure. Harris has contacted KMBC 9 Investigates and is urging other affected neighbors to speak up.

feed.title: Delta joins the growing list of US airlines raising checked bag fees as jet fuel costs soar
authors: RIO YAMAT — AP Airlines and Travel Writer
feed.summary: Delta Air Lines is raising its checked baggage fees, following similar moves by United Airlines and JetBlue, in response to soaring jet fuel prices attributed to the conflict in the Middle East. Most domestic and short-haul international passengers will now pay $45 for the first bag and $55 for the second, an increase of $10 each. This fee hike is part of Delta's strategy to offset a $400 million increase in operating expenses due to higher fuel costs.

feed.title: Did BofA Upgrade and New Accounting Chief Just Reframe State Street's (STT) Governance Narrative?
feed.summary: State Street Corporation has appointed C. Jack Read as its new Executive Vice President, Global Controller, and Chief Accounting Officer. This appointment, coupled with a recent BofA Securities rating upgrade, could influence the company's financial reporting and overall investment narrative. The article suggests that while Read's appointment may not immediately alter the focus on expense control and capital returns, it could impact how State Street navigates regulatory complexities and evolving digital market infrastructure, especially with its new Digital Asset Platform.

feed.title: Is It Too Late To Consider Leonardo DRS (DRS) After Its Strong 48% One-Year Rally?
feed.summary: Leonardo DRS (DRS) has rallied 48% over the past year, prompting an analysis of its current valuation. Using a Discounted Cash Flow model and Price-to-Earnings ratio, Simply Wall St suggests the stock might be overvalued by approximately 16.8%, as its current P/E of 44.17x is higher than both the industry average and its proprietary Fair Ratio. The article encourages investors to use personalized narratives to assess the stock's future value.

feed.title: Belgian Court Ruling Extends Pfizer Covid Cash Flow And Legal Questions
feed.summary: A recent Belgian court ruling has ordered Poland and Romania to honor their multi-billion euro COVID-19 vaccine contracts with Pfizer/BioNTech, requiring them to take delivery and pay for contracted volumes. This decision provides a boost to Pfizer's near-term cash flow visibility from legacy COVID contracts, reinforcing the enforceability of pandemic-era procurement deals. However, the ruling is specific to one jurisdiction and doesn't negate broader pressures like declining COVID-related revenue, higher debt, and ongoing legal challenges such as the Paxlovid patent appeal.

feed.title: John Dorfman: Humana, Thor hit Casualty List
feed.summary: Financial columnist John Dorfman presents his "Casualty List" of five stocks that have been unjustly punished and are poised for revival, despite significant drops in the first quarter. He recommends Humana, Cognizant Technology Solutions, Abercrombie & Fitch, Harmony Biosciences, and Thor Industries, highlighting their potential based on valuation and specific industry dynamics. Dorfman also reviews the strong performance of his previous year's Casualty List, which significantly outpaced the S&P 500.

feed.title: Enovix (ENVX) CEO Talluri receives 697,211-share RSU award with multi-year vesting
feed.summary: Enovix Corp (ENVX) CEO Rajendra K. Talluri has been granted 697,211 restricted stock units (RSUs) with a multi-year vesting schedule, reported in a recent Form 4 filing. These RSUs will vest in 12 equal quarterly installments starting April 1, 2026, contingent upon his continued service. Following this award, Talluri's total direct holdings and rights amount to 3,115,756 shares, which includes various vested and unvested performance RSUs set to release between 2027 and 2028.

feed.title: Community Health Systems to Webcast Q1 2026 Call
feed.summary: Community Health Systems, Inc. will webcast its first quarter 2026 conference call on April 30, 2026, at 11:00 a.m. Eastern Time. The company will discuss its financial and operational results for the quarter ending March 31, 2026. This webcast provides important insights into the healthcare industry and the company's performance for investors and analysts.

feed.title: For Cryoport Insiders, Selling US$1.6m Of Shares Was A Smart Move
feed.summary: Insiders at Cryoport (NASDAQ: CYRX) sold US$1.6 million worth of shares over the last year, with Chairman Jerrell Shelton making the largest individual sale. This occurred despite recent stock gains, and the selling price was even below the current market value, suggesting insiders may have viewed a lower price as reasonable. While insiders still collectively own a notable 3.6% of the company, the consistent selling without any purchases raises concerns about their confidence in the stock's future performance.

feed.title: Dimensional U.S. Core Equity 2 ETF (DFAC) stock price, news, quote and history
feed.summary: This page provides comprehensive information on the Dimensional U.S. Core Equity 2 ETF (DFAC), including its current stock price, historical data, and an overview of its investment strategy. The ETF aims to invest in a broad and diverse group of equity securities of U.S. companies. Key financial metrics such as net assets, PE ratio, yield, and expense ratio are also presented, alongside performance comparisons with its category and sector weightings.

feed.title: Flushing (NASDAQ: FFIC) shareholders approve OceanFirst stock merger terms
feed.summary: Flushing Financial Corporation (NASDAQ: FFIC) shareholders have approved the proposed merger with OceanFirst Financial Corp., with 24,102,136 votes in favor. Each Flushing share will convert into 0.85 shares of OceanFirst common stock. While state banking regulators have granted approval, the merger still requires Federal Reserve approval and other customary closing conditions.

feed.title: INVESTOR ALERT: Gartner, Inc. (IT) Investors with Substantial Losses Have Opportunity to Lead Investor Class Action Lawsuit
feed.summary: Robbins Geller Rudman & Dowd LLP has announced a class action lawsuit against Gartner, Inc. (NYSE: IT) on behalf of investors who suffered substantial losses. The lawsuit alleges that Gartner and its executive officers made false and misleading statements regarding the company's contract value growth potential and Consulting segment revenue outlook, while downplaying risks. The firm is seeking lead plaintiffs for the lawsuit, which must be filed by May 18, 2026.

feed.title: Holland America Guests to Face Higher Daily Gratuities and Service Charges
feed.summary: Holland America Line is set to increase its daily gratuities and service charges for sailings departing on or after June 1, 2026. Daily gratuities will rise by $1 per person, per day, while service charges on beverages and restaurants will increase from 18% to 20%. This change follows a similar increase by sister line Carnival Cruise Line and aligns Holland America's rates with other major cruise lines.

feed.title: Wells Fargo Helps Drive Growth in West Charlotte with $6 Million
feed.summary: Wells Fargo announced a $6 million philanthropic investment in West Charlotte, North Carolina, building on its existing community engagement in the city. This funding will support six nonprofit organizations focused on increasing housing access, providing workforce training, and fostering small business growth. The initiative aims to deliver coordinated, community-driven solutions to promote economic mobility and strengthen the city.

feed.title: ENERGIZER HOLDINGS, INC. TO WEBCAST A DISCUSSION OF SECOND QUARTER FISCAL YEAR 2026 RESULTS ON MAY 5
feed.summary: Energizer Holdings, Inc. (NYSE: ENR) announced it will webcast a discussion of its Second Quarter Fiscal Year 2026 results before the market opens on May 5. The investor conference call, featuring CEO Mark LaVigne and CFO John Drabik, will begin at 10 a.m. ET. Interested parties can access the live webcast, earnings press release, and related materials on the company's investor relations website.

feed.title: Energizer will post Q2 results before market open, then webcast
feed.summary: Energizer Holdings (NYSE: ENR) will release its Second Quarter Fiscal Year 2026 results before market open on May 5, 2026. This will be followed by a live investor webcast and conference call at 10:00 a.m. ET, hosted by CEO Mark LaVigne and CFO John Drabik. The earnings release, prepared remarks, and presentation will be available on the company's website prior to market open.

feed.title: Car-Mart to close 42 dealerships, lay off staff
feed.summary: America's Car-Mart Inc. announced it will close 42 dealerships and lay off staff due to market challenges impacting its ability to establish a "non-recourse revolving warehouse credit facility." The closures will reduce the company's total dealerships from 136 to 94 across 12 states by April 14, affecting about 31% of its store count but only 18% of its customers. This decision follows a significant third-quarter loss of $76.71 million and is intended to protect the company's $1.5 billion finance receivables portfolio and ensure long-term health.

feed.title: S&P Global Expands U.S. Employee Benefits By Matching Federal Government Contributions to Child Savings Accounts
feed.summary: S&P Global announced it will match the U.S. government's $1,000 contributions to newly established Section 530A child savings accounts, also known as "Trump Accounts." This benefit targets eligible children born between January 1, 2025, and December 31, 2028, of S&P Global employees. The initiative aims to support employees' financial wellness and foster long-term economic security for the next generation through tax-advantaged savings.

feed.title: Microchip secures IEC 62443‑4‑1 ML2 cybersecurity certification
feed.summary: Microchip Technology has achieved IEC 62443-4-1 Maturity Level 2 (ML2) certification for its industrial automation and control systems, following an independent audit by UL Solutions. This certification validates that Microchip's product development processes adhere to "secure by design" requirements, integrating security across hardware and software development from inception to end-of-life. The accomplishment aims to enhance customer confidence, streamline cybersecurity assessments, and facilitate compliance with regulatory requirements like the upcoming EU Cyber Resilience Act.

feed.title: [Form 4] Workday, Inc. Insider Trading Activity
feed.summary: Workday, Inc.'s Chief Accounting Officer, Mark S. Garfield, reported a tax-related disposition of 2,284 Class A Common Stock shares on April 5, 2026, at $132.26 per share to cover tax withholding for vesting restricted stock units (RSUs). Following this transaction, Garfield directly holds 33,052 shares of Workday Class A Common Stock, which includes 25,474 RSUs. This transaction is categorized as a routine tax withholding and is considered to have a moderate impact and neutral sentiment.

feed.title: Workday (WDAY) product and tech president sells shares, keeps 165K stake
feed.summary: Workday's President of Product and Technology, Gerrit S. Kazmaier, sold 9,356 shares of Class A Common Stock for $128.39 per share under a pre-established Rule 10b5-1 trading plan. Additionally, 12,270 shares were withheld for tax obligations related to restricted stock unit vesting. Following these transactions, Kazmaier directly holds 165,074 shares, including 161,956 restricted stock units.

feed.title: SLB OneSubsea and Subsea7 Sign Collaboration Agreement with PETRONAS Suriname
feed.summary: SLB OneSubsea and Subsea7 have signed a three-year collaboration agreement with PETRONAS Suriname to focus on offshore oil and gas projects in Suriname. This partnership aims to leverage the combined expertise of the companies for subsea engineering, procurement, installation, and commissioning, as well as research and development. The collaboration is expected to drive innovation and enhance offshore energy development in Suriname.

feed.title: SLB To Collaborate With Petronas Suriname
feed.summary: SLB has signed an agreement with Petronas Suriname and Subsea Integration Alliance to develop resources in Suriname's frontier basin. This long-term collaboration aims to utilize innovative, cost-effective subsea solutions, accelerate field development, and improve project economics. Subsea Integration Alliance may provide full engineering, procurement, construction, installation, and commissioning (EPCIC) solutions, including subsea production systems and umbilical, riser, and flowline components for discoveries in Suriname.

feed.title: RTX Wins $708.94M Contract for Bomb Production and Test Equipmen
feed.summary: Raytheon Technologies Corporation (RTX) has secured a significant contract valued at up to $708.94 million for the production of small diameter bomb increment II and associated test equipment. The project is expected to be completed by March 6, 2030, and includes foreign military sales to several countries. RTX, a major player in the Aerospace & Defense industry, demonstrates strong financial performance but faces considerations like its Altman Z-Score within the grey area and ongoing insider selling trends.

feed.title: CN Energy Group (Nasdaq:CNEY) - Stock Analysis
feed.summary: This Simply Wall St analysis provides an overview of CN Energy Group (Nasdaq: CNEY), highlighting its current stock price, market cap, and various financial metrics. The report identifies significant risks, including a short cash runway, small market cap, shareholder dilution, and declining earnings. It also details recent news and updates, noting substantial share price volatility and underperformance compared to the US Chemicals industry and broader market.

feed.title: Gap Insider Sells $628,500 in Stock
feed.summary: Gap Inc. insider Amanda Thompson sold 25,000 shares of the company's stock for $628,500, reducing her ownership by over 22%. This significant sale could indicate concerns about Gap's future performance or a personal investment diversification strategy, prompting investors to closely monitor the company. The transaction was disclosed in an SEC filing, and Gap stock closed down $0.63 at $24.72 following the sale.

feed.title: Insider Sell: Amanda Thompson Sells 25,000 Shares of Gap Inc (GA
feed.summary: Amanda Thompson, Chief People Officer at Gap Inc (GAP), sold 25,000 shares of the company on April 6, 2026. After this transaction, she now holds 86,228 shares. The company's stock was trading at $25.14, and is considered modestly overvalued with a price-to-GF-Value ratio of 1.16.

feed.title: Workday (NASDAQ: WDAY) CFO trims stake with 6,000-share sale
feed.summary: Workday, Inc. CFO Zane Rowe sold 6,000 shares of Class A Common Stock on April 7, 2026, through open-market transactions, as part of a pre-arranged Rule 10b5-1 trading plan. These sales occurred at weighted average prices between $127.6988 and $129.2601 per share. Additionally, 9,140 shares were withheld on April 5, 2026, to cover tax obligations related to vesting restricted stock units, which is a standard administrative procedure and not a discretionary market sale.

feed.title: Schall Law Firm Investigates DICK'S Sporting Goods
feed.summary: The Schall Law Firm is investigating DICK'S Sporting Goods (NYSE: DKS) for potential breaches of fiduciary duty by its directors and management. This investigation could lead to a securities class action lawsuit, aiming to recover damages for investors who owned DKS stock during the relevant period. Investors are encouraged to contact the firm to discuss their rights and potential participation in the investigation.

feed.title: Duffield trust in Workday (WDAY) converts, then sells 107,500 shares
feed.summary: David A. Duffield's revocable living trust converted 107,500 shares of Workday Class B Common Stock into Class A Common Stock and subsequently sold all 107,500 Class A shares in open-market transactions. These sales, executed under a pre-arranged Rule 10b5-1 trading plan, occurred at weighted average prices ranging from approximately $129 to $132 per share. Following these transactions, Duffield's trust retains significant holdings in both Class A and Class B Workday common stock.

feed.title: Newmont Shares Rise 1.5% Amid Analyst Optimism
feed.summary: Newmont Corporation (NYSE:NEM) saw its shares climb 1.5% due to positive sentiment from Wall Street analysts. Several firms, including Goldman Sachs and Royal Bank of Canada, raised their price targets and maintained buy ratings, indicating strong financial performance and growth prospects for the gold mining giant. Investors are now anticipating Newmont's upcoming quarterly earnings report for further insights.

feed.title: A SLB OneSubsea e a Subsea7 assinam acordo de colaboração com a PETRONAS Suriname
feed.summary: SLB OneSubsea and Subsea7 have signed a strategic collaboration agreement with PETRONAS Suriname E&P B.V. to accelerate the development of subsea oil and gas resources in Suriname's frontier basin. This long-term partnership aims to provide integrated subsea solutions, reducing risks, shortening lead times, and maximizing value for PETRONAS Suriname. The collaboration focuses on enhancing project performance, safety, and achieving sustainable outcomes through innovative and cost-effective subsea solutions.

feed.title: Citigroup Maintains Buy on AMZN (Amazon.com, Inc.) March 17, 2026
feed.summary: Citigroup maintained a "Buy" rating on Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN) on March 17, 2026, indicating continued confidence in its core e-commerce and cloud sectors, despite a muted short-term price reaction. While Wells Fargo set a higher price target of $305.00, DA Davidson reduced its target to $175, reflecting varied analyst opinions. A maintained Buy primarily suggests stable fundamentals and revenue growth, rather than new bullish momentum, urging investors to consider operational improvements in delivery and AWS margins.

feed.title: Trump Proposes $1.5T Defense Budget: 4 Stocks To Watch - Northrop Grumman (NYSE:NOC)
feed.summary: President Donald Trump's proposed $1.5 trillion fiscal 2027 defense budget is expected to significantly impact the aerospace and defense sector. UBS highlights RTX Corporation, General Dynamics, and Huntington Ingalls Industries as potential beneficiaries due to increased funding for missiles and shipbuilding. Conversely, Northrop Grumman is identified as a potential loser, as the budget unexpectedly trims the B-21 Raider stealth bomber program.
